createGame(uint256,uint256,address[]).custom:security: This function is public and can be called by anyone
createGame(uint256,uint256,address[]).details: This function creates a new game and assigns it a unique ID. The game is stored      in the games mapping and indexed by the creator's address. The function adds the      caller and any provided players to the game's player list.

params.levelId: The ID of the level to play
params.players: Array of additional player addresses to add to the game (can be empty)
params.wagerAmount: The amount to wager for this game

returns.gameId: The unique identifier of the newly created game

createLevel(string,(uint8,uint8)[]).custom:security: This function is public and can be called by anyone.
createLevel(string,(uint8,uint8)[]).details: This function creates a new level and assigns it a unique ID. The level is stored      in the levels mapping and indexed by the owner's address. The function emits a      LevelCreated event upon successful creation.

params.map: An array of Block structs representing the level's topology
params.name: The human-readable name for the level

returns.levelId: The unique identifier of the newly created level

getLevelBlocks(uint256).details: This function allows external callers to retrieve the full block map for a level.      It is necessary because Solidity's public getter for structs containing dynamic arrays      does not return the entire array, only individual elements by index.

params.levelId: The ID of the level to retrieve blocks for

returns._0: An array of Block structs representing the level's map

params.owner: The address to get level IDs for

returns._0: An array of level IDs owned by the address

getTeamNames(address).custom:security: This function is public and can be called by anyone to view any address's team names
getTeamNames(address).details: This function retrieves the team names for a given address from the teams mapping.      If the address has no team, it will return an empty array.

params.player: The address to get team names for

returns._0: An array of strings containing the hotdog names in the player's team

setTeamNames(string[]).custom:security: This function can only be called by the team ownerThis function will revert if array length is not 4 or if any name is empty
setTeamNames(string[]).details: This function allows a player to set their team's hotdog names.      The function requires exactly 4 names to be provided in the array.      Each name must be non-empty.      The function will revert if any of these conditions are not met.

params.names: Array of exactly 4 hotdog names for the team

createGame(uint256,uint256,address[]).notice: Creates a new game with the specified level, wager amount, and players

createLevel(string,(uint8,uint8)[]).notice: Creates a new level with the given name and block map

getLevelBlocks(uint256).notice: Returns the array of Block structs for a given level ID

getOwnerLevels(address).notice: Returns an array of level IDs owned by the specified address

getTeamNames(address).notice: Returns the array of hotdog names for a specific address's team

setTeamNames(string[]).notice: Sets the team names for the caller's team
